1. Pink bar: 60 → 59 pixels (shrunk)
2. White region id=2: 1048 → 972 pixels, boundary changed from `[[1, 55], [19, 55]]` to `[[1, 51], [19, 51]]` - the right edge moved left from col 55 to col 51

4. White region id=7: 96 → 160 pixels, boundary changed from `[[24, 7], [39, 7]]` to `[[24, 11], [39, 11]]` - the right edge moved right from col 7 to col 11

Wait, this is interesting. The white region id=7 expanded from width 4 (cols 4-7) to width 8 (cols 4-11). But the blue blocks are at cols 0-3. So the white region expanded to the right, into the gray area.

- Blue blocks are on the LEFT side (cols 0-3)
- White regions are to the RIGHT of the blue blocks
- The gray region is the main background
When I click a blue block, the white region next to it expands to the right. This seems like the blue blocks are "pushing" the white regions.

Actually, wait. Let me re-examine. The blue block at rows 24-27 is adjacent to the white region id=7. When I clicked it, the white region expanded. But the blue block itself didn't move - it's still at cols 0-3.
So the mechanic seems to be: clicking a blue block expands the white region adjacent to it. The goal might be to expand all white regions to fill the gray area.

Actually, id=2's boundary was `[[1, 0], [1, 55], [19, 55], [19, 4], [16, 4], [15, 3], [15, 0]]` and now is `[[1, 0], [1, 51], [19, 51], [19, 4], [16, 4], [15, 3], [15, 0]]`. The right edge moved from col 55 to col 51.

So id=2 shrank by 4 columns on the right, and id=7 expanded by 4 columns on the right. This suggests that the gray region is being "pushed" from the right side when I click the blue block.
Hmm, but why would clicking a blue block affect the white region at the top? Let me think about connectivity.

But the white region id=2 at the top also changed. Its right edge moved from col 55 to col 51. This might be because the gray region expanded on the right side, pushing the white region id=2 to the left.
